(各務原市川島笠田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q124668655}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shirahige Shrine |Image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirohigejinjakakamigaharashikawashimakasada}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:各務原市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

(各務原市川島笠田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q124668655}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shirahige Shrine |Image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shirahige-jinja (worship)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shirahige-jinja (worship)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shirahige-jinja (worship)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

(各務原市川島笠田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q124668655}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shirahige Shrine |Image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shirahige Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shirahige-jinja (worship)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

(各務原市川島笠田町)]] {{wikidata link|Q124668655}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shirahige Shrine |Image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}Kawashima Kasada-chō 146 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Shirahige Shrine''' (しろひげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kasada-chō|Q131938355|WD=Q131938355|1=Kawashima Kasada-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} ({{ill|lt=Shirahige Shrine|Q705303|WD=Q705303|1=Shirahige Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was formerly classified as a village shrine. It is designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsa) for Kawashima Shrine of Kawashima in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1179–1181}}The Owari-shi (a local gazetteer compiled by order of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|WD=Q7633|1=1844}})) records that “the Junior Third Rank Kawashima Tenjin of Haguri District listed in the Engishiki is the Shirahige Shrine of Kasada-jima Village.”. Other candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Enoki Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q134893091|WD=Q134893091|lt=Enoki Shrine|1=Enoki Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|WD=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, Kakamigahara City, and {{ill|lt=Kawashima Shrine (Kōnan)|Q11478285|WD=Q11478285|lt=Kawashima Shrine|1=Kawashima Shrine}} in Miyata-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q820496|WD=Q820496|1=Kōnan, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (さるたひこのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) === History === [[File:Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (2).jpg|thumb|Main hall built atop high stone walls]] The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was established in the 10th year of the {{ill|lt=Mizunoe-Ne|Q904972|WD=Q904972|1=Yang Water Rat}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} following an imperial decreeIf taken as the 10th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, this would correspond to 322., and a shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|lt=Kinoto-U|Q904791|WD=Q904791|1=Yin Wood Rabbit}} era of Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Ingyō|Q349258|WD=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}} following another imperial decreeIf taken as the 4th year of Emperor Ingyō’s reign, this would correspond to 415.Explanatory signboard “History of Shirahige Shrine.”{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}} (717), Hachiman Shrine was established as a subordinate shrine within the precinctsFrom the inscription on the monument marking the former site of Hachiman Shrine within the grounds of the Inaba District Gymnasium, Kakamigahara City.{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 14 (1586), flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} washed away the shrine buildings. As a result of this flood, a new course of the Kiso River became the boundary between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and this area changed from Haguri District, Owari Province, to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q11610214|WD=Q11610214|1=Haguri district}}, Mino Province. The subordinate Hachiman Shrine was relocated to Majima in Haguri District (present-day {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kamiochi-chō, at the site of the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium|Q109603254|WD=Q109603254|1=Kakamigahara City Inaba District Gymnasium}}). Thereafter, it was moved to its present location in Kasada, Haguri District, by {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi Toshisada|Q11425729|WD=Q11425729|1=Tsubouchi Toshisada}}, a {{ill|lt=Hatamoto|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} of high rank stationed at {{ill|lt=Shinkano Jinya|Q11501438|WD=Q11501438|1=Shinkanō Jin'ya}} (former lord of {{ill|lt=Matsukura Castle (Owari Province)|Q11239240|WD=Q11239240|lt=Matsukura Castle|1=Matsukura Castle}}){{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. It was renamed Shirahige Myōjin and later changed to Shirahige Shrine. During the Meiji period, it became a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Shirohei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. In 1999 (Heisei 11), the worship hall, norito hall, main hall, {{ill|lt=Heibei|Q124160302|WD=Q124160302|1=banpei}}, and other structures were newly built, and Shinmei Shrine was enshrined togetherThe former location was 1266 Koyabajima, Kasada-chō. It is now within {{ill|lt=Kasen Kankyō Rakuen|Q11553849|WD=Q11553849|1=Water Eco Park}}.. Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (4).jpg|Subordinate shrine Shirahige-jinja Kawashima ac (3).jpg|Purification font === Subordinate shrines === Originally, there were two subordinate shrines: Kaijin Shrine and Akiba Shrine{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|p=1174}}. In 1996 (Heisei 8), nearby shrines were absorbed and incorporated as subordinate shrines. * Kaijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Sokotsu Watatsumi-no-Kami|1=Watatsumi}})From the on-site information board of “Shirahige Shrine.” * Akiba Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Suijin Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}})A shrine enshrined at the site of a levee breach caused by flooding. It was later relocated for river improvement and levee construction. In this area, Suijin Shrines sometimes enshrine deities other than {{ill|lt=Suijin|Q3137449|WD=Q3137449|1=Suijin}} (such as Amaterasu), possibly because they also serve the purpose of warding off flood damage. * Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}) * Konpira Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}) === Other === * {{ill|lt=Chūkonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Memorial monument for the war dead|1=monument to the loyal dead}} === Access === * From the “Kasada Higashiguchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line, an 8-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shirahige-jinja (Kakamigahara)}} * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=142 Shirahige Shrine] – Gifu Prefecture Association of
